item.title: STAFF POST- Open Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Show Reasoning
Hide Reasoning

Crosslink to other spaceborne anomalies which actively convey communication.

Editing this:

Radiation typically consists of radio waves, but has on at least two occasions included visible light.

To this:

Radiation typically consists of radio waves1, but has on at least two occasions included visible light.

Footnotes
1. SCP-2548's relationship to other spaceborne, transmitting anomalies, such as SCP-2821 and SCP-1548 is currently unknown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Carbon book. It called Agent Tanser a carbon book. That is so unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After 12 hours searching for this one because it lacks the sapient / Sentient tags…
Is there a reason why it's 1548, but friendly? Loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there any reason why it hasn't been tagged sapient or/and sentient?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaaaaw, it just want to make friends. I want to hug it and have nice, long talk with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noooooo sad space entity nooooooooo. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Durendal reaches Earth and successfully splashes down. Agent Kerns is awarded the Foundation Cross and Agent Tanser receives a posthumous commendation for sacrifice in the line of duty.

Nice touch, don't see enough of this kind of thing in articles. Otherwise… Are we to assume that the Voyager 1 fell "prey"1 to the anomaly, or that the Voyager 1 somehow created the anomaly? From the beginning, I thought it was the latter, but the way the thing acted made me doubt that.

Footnotes
1. Don't really want to use that word, because the skip's so cute and nice, but I didn't have a better one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww man, that last bit… +1 for unexpectedly giving me the feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You hooked my attention at "Misinformation efforts are to include assurances that the Voyager 1 space probe is still operational, with associated false evidence."

I like SCP-2548's dialogue a lot. You can tell it's doing a lot of the same things we are here- encountering a very strange form of life and trying its best to establish peace and understanding with limited information. (I wonder what information it encoded on those "iron lungs", and how we would read it if we could…)

(The Foundation could learn a lot from their operatives within other space programs, I think. If your unmanned probe ceases to function in a manner you don't understand, "send people after it" should NOT be your immediate next step. :P It's perfectly in character for them, though- good work there.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In regards to your link… shh, no-one needs to know…

The Foundation most likely decided that, if it's quiet when we ignore it, we should keep ignoring it. This could have consequences, of course...

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Beautifully done. Now I'm curious too; why didn't the Foundation reply?

Also, I'm just going to leave this here…FYI…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if I should feel bad for it or be scared of it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me so sad. Foundation, why you no talk it it? It's friendly for pete sake!
Nonetheless, my feels make me give +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Car broke, phone yes?

This thing is absolutely adorable, even if it effectively killed someone…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh oh oh. Oh it is so good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. Space god buddy.
Also, foundation space exploration and the spaceship returned without exploding in six different dimensions and two parallel timelines?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, I must have made an error in a very early incarnation of this article (parts of the description were actually drafted months ago, the rest was added only in the past week or so). I'll fix that. I'm glad you like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Durendal's radio begins receiving messages. The radio signal does not originate from outside the cabin.

"Oh shit" moment right there. I kind of feel bad for this thing, though. It's so lonely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An awesome entry, congratulations.

However, is there a special reason you excluded Uranus in the 25 AU quote?
Well, at 25 AU it _is_ surrounded by Saturn and Neptune, but Uranus is the next neighbour on the Saturn side, or am I mistaken?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The site lacks space-themed skips and tales.

You can always check for SCPs with the "extraterrestrial" tag!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For some reason this gave me shivers. Maybe it's because how human and at the same time alien the skips sounds. Or maybe just because the incident log reminded me of 2001: A Space Odyssey.

Great piece. The site lacks space-themed skips and tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to all who helped get this out: Greenwolf, Devereaux, EldritchCyanide, Minmin, salvagebar, LadyKatie, Dmatix, TwistedGears, Illustrio, Doctor Cimmerian, and more.

Art by SunnyClockwork
Art by SunnyClockwork
